species_absent_note: These 28 species are absent from train.csv (train_audio). Some may have labeled examples in train_soundscapes_labels.csv per official competition description. Verify before declaring zero-shot.

train_audio.domain_match_to_test: low — single-species XC/iNat recordings, different acoustic environment
train_audio.quality_field: rating (1-5 for XC, 0 for unrated, absent for iNat)

train_soundscapes_labeled.domain_match_to_test: high — same recording locations as test_soundscapes
train_soundscapes_labeled.note: Some species may ONLY appear here and not in train_audio. Critical for species coverage.

train_soundscapes_unlabeled.domain_match_to_test: high — same recording locations as test_soundscapes
train_soundscapes_unlabeled.note: Primary target for pseudo-labeling. Soundscapes without entries in train_soundscapes_labels.csv.

splits.validation: group-aware from train metadata (author or site-based)

label_format.submission: float probability per species per 5s window

benchmark_targets.metric_confidence: working_assumption — inferred from float-probability submission format + 2024-2025 precedent

notes: Multi-label soundscape classification: 5-second windows, 234 species columns, float probabilities.
28 species absent from train_audio — verify coverage in train_soundscapes_labels.csv before declaring zero-shot.
train_soundscapes share recording locations with test — highest domain match, must be used for training.
Extreme class imbalance in train_audio: Aves = 97.9%, Reptilia = 1 sample.
Training audio is OGG format, resampled to 32 kHz.
Use group-aware validation based on author or site metadata to reduce leakage.
rating field (XC only) available for sample quality weighting.
latitude/longitude available for geographic diversity analysis.
Insect sonotypes (e.g., 47158son16) are treated as classes despite lack of species-level ID.
Official: 'we expect there is no benefit to looking for more on xeno-canto.org or iNaturalist'.
